title: GxP App Manifest
description: Plugin metadata + default datastore values. Read by the platform at install time and by the toolkit dev server.

asset_dir.description: Relative path to the asset directory, e.g. /src/public.

formTemplate.description: Declares this plugin as a form/quiz/survey app. When true, the platform expects `configuration.json` to include a `formTemplate` root key — an array of cards defining the starter question set an admin can customize on install.

settings.description: Default key/value settings (pluginVars). Keys should match identifiers used in gxp-settings directives.

strings.description: i18n strings. Must contain a `default` locale map; other keys are locale codes.

assets.description: Default asset URLs/paths keyed by gxp-src identifier.

triggerState.description: Default values for dynamic state updated by sockets/CLI.

form.description: Mock form definition for form-backed apps. When present (or when settings.formId is set), the dev store attaches a gxpFormStore as store.form with the same interface plugins get on-platform (getSections/getElements, formData, validation, submit).

formId.description: Form slug/id. Falls back to settings.formId, then 'dev-form'.

schema.description: Form schema: either v2 shape { root, cards, elements } or { sections: [...] } with fields arrays.

sections.description: Plain nested sections array ({ title, fields, sections }) — alternative to schema.

settings.description: Form settings object (registration_mode etc.), as returned by the platform.

prefillData.description: Field slug → value map seeded into formData (simulates attendee prefill).

conditions.description: Enable conditional-visibility processing of condition_params against formData.

mockResponses.description: Canned results returned by store.form.submit()/saveProgress() instead of hitting an API.

track-events.description: Analytics events reported via gxp-track / window.gxp.track, used by the platform for reporting. Keys are event identifiers (e.g. checkin.submit); values map each gxp-track-props root key to a type: the string "string" for free-form values, an array of allowed values, or a { type, value } object for relationship values (e.g. { "type": "attendee", "value": "id" }).

value.description: Field of the related model the prop carries, e.g. id.
